root_text.sn55.12:1.4: ‘ehi tvaṁ, ambho purisa, kālasseva uṭṭhāya pācīnamukho yāhi.
root_text.sn55.12:1.5: So tvaṁ mā sobbhaṁ parivajjehi, mā papātaṁ, mā khāṇuṁ, mā kaṇḍakaṭhānaṁ, mā candaniyaṁ, mā oḷigallaṁ.
root_text.sn55.12:1.7: Evaṁ tvaṁ, ambho purisa, kāyassa bhedā paraṁ maraṇā sugatiṁ saggaṁ lokaṁ upapajjissasī’ti.
root_text.sn55.12:2.1: Taṁ kho panetaṁ, bhikkhave, brāhmaṇānaṁ bālagamanametaṁ mūḷhagamanametaṁ na nibbidāya na virāgāya na nirodhāya na upasamāya na abhiññāya na sambodhāya na nibbānāya saṁvattati.
root_text.sn55.12:2.2: Ahañca kho, bhikkhave, ariyassa vinaye udayagāminiṁ paṭipadaṁ paññāpemi;
root_text.sn55.12:2.3: yā ekantanibbidāya virāgāya nirodhāya upasamāya abhiññāya sambodhāya nibbānāya saṁvattati.
root_text.sn55.12:3.1: Katamā ca sā, bhikkhave, udayagāminī paṭipadā;
root_text.sn55.12:3.3: Idha, bhikkhave, ariyasāvako buddhe aveccappasādena samannāgato hoti—
root_text.sn55.12:3.4: itipi so bhagavā arahaṁ sammāsambuddho …pe… satthā devamanussānaṁ buddho bhagavāti;
root_text.sn55.12:3.7: ariyakantehi sīlehi samannāgato hoti akhaṇḍehi …pe… samādhisaṁvattanikehi.
root_text.sn55.12:3.8: Ayaṁ kho sā, bhikkhave, udayagāminī paṭipadā ekantanibbidāya …pe…

translation_text.sn55.12:1.2: “Mendicants, the brahmins advocate a practice called ‘get up and go’.
translation_text.sn55.12:1.4: ‘Please, good people, rising early you should face east and walk.
translation_text.sn55.12:1.5: Do not avoid a pit, a cliff, a stump, thorny ground, a swamp, or a sewer.
translation_text.sn55.12:1.6: You should await death in the place that you fall.
translation_text.sn55.12:1.7: And when your body breaks up, after death, you’ll be reborn in a good place, a heaven realm.’
translation_text.sn55.12:2.1: But this practice of the brahmins is a foolish procedure, a stupid procedure. It doesn’t lead to disillusionment, dispassion, cessation, peace, insight, awakening, or extinguishment.
translation_text.sn55.12:2.2: But in the training of the Noble One I advocate a ‘get up and go’ practice
translation_text.sn55.12:2.3: which does lead solely to disillusionment, dispassion, cessation, peace, insight, awakening, and extinguishment.
translation_text.sn55.12:3.1: And what is that ‘get up and go’ practice?
translation_text.sn55.12:3.3: It’s when a noble disciple has experiential confidence in the Buddha …
translation_text.sn55.12:3.7: And they have the ethical conduct loved by the noble ones … leading to immersion.
translation_text.sn55.12:3.8: This is that ‘get up and go’ practice
translation_text.sn55.12:3.9: which does lead solely to disillusionment, dispassion, cessation, peace, insight, awakening, and extinguishment.”

variant_text.sn55.12:1.5: kaṇḍakaṭhānaṁ → kaṇḍakādhānaṁ (bj); kaṇṭakaṭṭhānaṁ (sya-all); kaṇṭakaṁ ṭhānaṁ (pts1ed); kaṇḍakaṁ ṭhānaṁ (mr) | candaniyaṁ → candanikaṁ (bj, sya-all) | oḷigallaṁ → oligallaṁ (bj)
